title: parse --json (AST/locations)
manager: `parse --json` emits the parsed AST with source locations as JSON, so any tool in any language can consume FSL structure without embedding the parser. This is the integration primitive: editors, linters, doc generators, and codegen all get a stable, machine-readable view of a machine's syntax tree, with locations precise enough to underline the exact offending token rather than the whole line.
cs: `parse --json` emits AST + source locations; the interop surface for external tooling; locations feed diagnostics and editor features.
